pattern: Every guess partitions the surviving candidates by how many letters lined up, so choosing the guess whose partition is least self-similar shrinks the pool fastest instead of just eliminating one word at a time.
hints: Guessing candidates in list order can leave a large pool untouched if the response happens to be uninformative round after round.
After each guess, keep only the words that would have produced the exact same match count against that guess — everything else is provably not the secret.

Prefer a guess with few or no candidates that share zero letters in common with it: a word isolated from the rest of the pool wastes a whole round if it turns out wrong, so favor guesses that are 'central' to the remaining candidates.

edge_inputs.description: a single-word list — the only candidate is the secret
small pool of four dissimilar words
ten-word pool where an uninformative first guess would waste rounds
sixteen-word pool, a representative mid-size case
twenty-word pool, the largest tested — stresses convergence speed
adversarial pool of near-duplicates, each differing from the secret by exactly one letter

stress.description: random pool of distinct 6-letter lowercase words with a random secret
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    n = max(1, min(size, 16))
    words = set()
    while len(words) < n:
        words.add(''.join(rng.choice(string.ascii_lowercase) for _ in range(6)))
    words = list(words)
    secret = rng.choice(words)
    return [words, {'secret': secret, 'words': words, 'budget': 10}]
